cards.heading: TDS
cards.content: Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) is a mechanism where the person making a payment, called the deductor, deducts a certain percentage of tax before paying the recipient, known as the deductee.

cards.heading: TCS
cards.content: TCS (Tax Collected at Source) is the tax collected by the seller from the buyer during a transaction.

cards.heading: taxes-paid
cards.content: You are a resident and have Foreign Assets or Income or you have paid taxes outside India.

cards.heading: taxes-filling
cards.content: These sections are not required in most cases.  If any of these details are applicable to you, enter the details below. Else click Go to Next.

cards.heading: upload-form-16
cards.content: Your Form 16 data is safely stored and will remain available even if you

cards.heading: GSTR-1
cards.content: Outward Supplies Are The Sales Of Goods & Services Furnished By All Registered Taxpayers.

cards.heading: GSTR-2A
cards.content: When The Purchase Of The Registered Taxpayer Has Been Done, The Information Of Their Sales Reflect In Monthly GSTR-2A.

cards.heading: GSTR-2B
cards.content: An Auto Drafted Sale Of A Registered Tax Payer.

cards.heading: GSTR-3B
cards.content: A Consolidated Summary Of Inward And Outward Flow Of Sale And Purchase.

cards.heading: GSTR-4
cards.content: A Registered Entity Opting For A Composition, Needs To File Quarterly Return In A Year.

cards.heading: GSTR-7
cards.content: A Monthly Return To Be Filed By An Individual Who's TDS Has Been Deducted Under GST Or TDS Under Goods & Supply Tax.

cards.heading: Accounting
cards.content: Accounting is the process of recording and managing financial transactions to track income, expenses, and profit, helping businesses understand their financial position and make decisions.

cards.heading: Audit 44AD
cards.content: Under Section 44AD of the Income Tax Act, small businesses can declare income at a fixed percentage of turnover. If they show lower income, a tax audit is required.

cards.heading: Audit 44ADA
cards.content: Under Section 44ADA of the Income Tax Act, professionals can declare 50% of their receipts as income on a presumptive basis. If they show lower income, a tax audit is required.

cards.heading: Audit 44AE
cards.content: Under Section 44AE of the Income Tax Act, transporters can declare income on a fixed amount per vehicle basis. If they report lower income than the prescribed amount, a tax audit may be required.
